description_long: This house features a series of staggered, block-like forms that step upward in a geometric, terraced fashion, resembling a modernist architectural approach. The exterior is uniformly finished in a pale, mint-green color, with sharp, angular lines and flat surfaces dominating the structure. The roofline is composed of multiple flat planes at varying heights, creating a dynamic, multi-level appearance. There are no visible windows or doors, giving the house a monolithic and abstract quality. The overall massing suggests a compact, single-family residence with a strong emphasis on horizontal and vertical planes. The structure appears to be constructed from solid, dense material, likely concrete or stone, with a matte, slightly rough finish.
description: A modern, terraced house with blocky, stepped forms and a pale mint-green exterior, featuring flat roofs and sharp geometric lines. The structure is monolithic and windowless, emphasizing a minimalist, abstract design.
description_view_0: The front view shows the stepped, block-like facade with the highest sections toward the right, highlighting the geometric layering.
description_view_1: This side view reveals the depth of the terraced steps and the solid, angular massing of the house.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the full width of the stepped roofline and the uniform, flat surfaces across the structure.
description_view_3: This opposite side view emphasizes the lower, receding steps and the consistent blocky geometry.
attribute_entryway_design: no visible entryway; monolithic block form with no distinct door or porch
